title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Stakeholder Model Mission
summary: A municipal utility analyst. The analyst needs an explainable rate and assistance model under revenue and affordability constraints. Planned artifact: Deliver Equitable Utility Tariff Model with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Turn stakeholder needs into answerable model questions, quantities, constraints, and validation criteria.
Model thresholds and distance with absolute-value and piecewise relationships.
Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Linear Decision applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.
